# tokens: # typeof / typepathof — inspecting a value's type
# `typeof v` returns the type of `v` as a Type literal:
#   - a concrete value → its exact VType (Integer, ProperString, …);
#   - a type literal → its metatype: a Scalar.. subtype → ScalarType,
#     a Node.. subtype → NodeType, an Object.. subtype → ObjectType,
#     and the abstract roots (Any, Scalar, Node, Object) → Type;
#   - `typeof typeof v` therefore reports the metatype.
# `typepathof v` returns the ancestry path of `typeof v` as a List of
# Type literals, root first, leaf last — the last element always
#   firstq  — [List] forward-prec — first element or None

note: a non-empty string is a ProperString
a single space is still non-empty
the empty string is an EmptyString
a quoted word is an Atom
the unique inhabitant of None reports None
a def-bound reference resolves to the bound value first
a list of mixed nodes is still a List

# tokens: # 3. typeof — type literals report their metatype

note: the abstract Node root → Type (not NodeType)
the abstract Scalar root → Type (not ScalarType)
the abstract Object root → Type (not ObjectType)

# tokens: typeof fn [ [ a:Integer ] [ Integer ] [ a addq 1 ] ]

note: an enum builds a Disjunct type value
a value satisfies its own type
a type literal → the path of its metatype
the result is itself a List
every scalar path is rooted at Scalar
node paths are rooted at Node
the root element is the Scalar type literal
Scalar is not a Number — subtyping runs the other way
the whole path matches a literal template of type literals
